factors.name: Dow earnings & guidance
factors.summary: SoftBank surging 11%+ on AI optimism, Lenovo +13% on AI earnings; Boeing eying delivery rebound. Mixed signal—AI tailwinds vs. aerospace/industrial headwinds (Boeing delays, SpaceX scrubs).

factors.name: Fed policy & rate expectations
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed chair Friday (hawkish lean vs Blankfein era). Rising talk of hawkish Fed tone per FXStreet; USD at 6-week high (DXY 99.25). Rate-sensitive Dow names (utilities, dividend payers) under pressure.

factors.name: US-Iran ceasefire progress & oil
factors.summary: Oil down ~$1 on peace talk optimism (WTI $96.80, Brent mid-$80s expected per Bloomberg survey). Eases energy shock inflation fears and reduces geopolitical risk premium. Supports consumer & transport costs.

factors.name: Japan CPI & BOJ
factors.summary: Japan core CPI 1.4% YoY (4-year low), well below 1.7% forecast, but ING still expects June BOJ hike. Soft inflation may delay BoJ tightening vs. near-term expectations; mixed for yen and global growth.

factors.name: China data & FDI policy
factors.summary: NDRC softens tone on foreign tech investment (no outright ban), but China's National Team cutting ETF stakes 90% in H1 2026—mixed liquidity signal. Caterpillar & materials demand outlook unclear.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD at 6-week high (DXY ~99.25) pressures multinational earnings translation; Dow has ~50% international revenue. EUR/USD weak at 1.1610, USD/JPY at 159.10; dollar carry-trade tightening.

factors.name: US labor & growth momentum
factors.summary: US resilient per 'robust labour data' note; Australia jobless rate up (RBA hike bets fade). Mixed global growth backdrop—no fresh US PMI/ISM data in this window.

factors.name: Geopolitical tensions
factors.summary: Trump sends 5,000 more troops to Poland (week after cancelling 4,000); Iran nuclear deal fragile; Alberta referendum on separation looms. Elevated tail risks & policy uncertainty.

factors.name: Valuation & market structure
factors.summary: MarketWatch warns 'Buffett Indicator' signals market top (230% valuation gap); technical sell signals flashing despite Nvidia optimism. Fragility narrative building despite upside narrative.

factors.name: Price action momentum
factors.summary: US30 +0.23% in 4h, +0.53% in 24h—modest rally underway but not yet extended. Move is in-progress (Phase 2), requires retest to confirm strength.

screening.narrative: The Dow is in a modest Phase 2 rally (+0.53% in 24h) on US-Iran ceasefire optimism and select mega-cap AI wins (SoftBank +11%, Lenovo +13%), offsetting hawkish Fed chair Warsh signals and persistent dollar strength (DXY 99.25). Japan's unexpectedly soft CPI (1.4% vs 1.7% expected) eases near-term BoJ tightening fears but also signals global growth slowness. However, valuation warnings ('Buffett Indicator' signals excess) and elevated geopolitical risk (troops to Poland, Iran deal fragility, Alberta referendum) create a conflict between bullish tactical catalysts and bearish structural headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: US Michigan Consumer Sentiment (May; due Friday AM before market close at 2 PM ET for bonds)
Memorial Day (markets closed Monday 5/25; shortened Friday; full reopening Tuesday 5/26)
RBNZ rate decision (May 27—expected hold at 2.25%, but Q3 hike expectations rising)
Dow component earnings flow (BA, CAT, GE, JPM, V guidance in coming weeks)
Fed Warsh inaugural commentary/signals (new chair tone-setting)
China macro (any PMI/growth data releases; NDRC/PBOC policy signals)
Oil/energy market developments (Iran deal closure vs. stall-out could swing +/- $10/barrel)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran talks collapse or geopolitical escalation spikes, oil could spike >$110/bbl, triggering inflation fears and Flight-to-Quality (bonds rally, equities dump). Conversely, if Fed Warsh cuts rates faster than expected or US-Iran deal finalizes, Dow rallies hard through 50,500. Valuation risk (Buffett Indicator warning) means upside is capped if growth disappoints or tech earnings disappoint. China's ETF liquidation may trigger unexpected liquidity drain.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (underway); wait for pullback retest of 50,150–50,200 support to confirm strength before buying. On break above 50,500, reassess—extended moves often fizzle. Avoid chasing here; risk/reward favors patience into Memorial Day holiday gap and next major catalyst (BoJ/PMI/earnings).

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Optimism
factors.summary: US Secretary of State Rubio signaled 'good signs' for agreement; multiple sources report draft proposals exchanged and mediation intensifying. Risk-on sentiment supports equity demand.

factors.name: Oil Price Pressure & Strait of Hormuz Risk
factors.summary: WTI trading mid-$96s with reports of lingering uranium stockpile disputes and Hormuz closure risks. Commerzbank warns crude could spike above recent highs if Strait remains closed; energy cost headwinds for industrial/transport heavy Dow names.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength (6-week high)
factors.summary: USD holding near 99.50 DXY on hawkish Fed expectations despite Iran peace optimism. Strong dollar pressures multinational earnings translation for ~50% Dow international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Stagflation Concerns & UK/EU Weakness
factors.summary: UK retail sales fell 1.3% MoM vs -0.6% expected; France business confidence at Feb 2021 lows; Danske Bank notes stagflation fears rising. European recession risks may dampen global demand for Dow industrials (CAT, BA, GE).

factors.name: Tech/AI Sector Momentum (Nvidia, SoftBank, Lenovo)
factors.summary: Nvidia earnings focus positive, SoftBank gained $61B market cap, Lenovo surged 15% on AI revenue doubling. Broad-based AI enthusiasm extends to large-cap names; however, not a Dow-heavy driver relative to industrials.

factors.name: Global Equity Risk Sentiment (Asia strength, EM gains)
factors.summary: Japan Nikkei +2.74%, Asian stocks rising, EM equities on track for weekly gain. Positive spillover on easing geopolitical jitters and peace deal hopes supports US equity flows.

factors.name: Japan CPI & BOJ Rate Hike Signal
factors.summary: Japan core inflation fell to 4-year low (1.5% vs 1.7% expected); however, ING expects June BOJ hike anyway due to pipeline price pressures. Tighter global monetary conditions weigh on risk appetite.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations (year-end)
factors.summary: Markets pricing higher probability of Fed hike by year-end on hawkish sentiment; real yields rising. Pressures dividend-heavy Dow names (UNH, V, KO, MCD) and rate-sensitive industrials.

factors.name: China Tech Regulation & Foreign Investment Scrutiny
factors.summary: NDRC and regulators questioning AI-fueled stock rallies and demanding disclosure from companies/funds. Supply-chain and growth uncertainty for global tech/industrial supply chains.

factors.name: Upcoming Data & Event Risk
factors.summary: German IFO Business Survey due today; no major US data releases flagged for immediate hours. Weekend approaching with mixed headlines; uncertainty caps conviction ahead of week-end close.

key_levels.resistance: 50,400–50,500 (recent intraday highs, Iran deal euphoria cap)

screening.narrative: US30 opened the week with jitters but has staged a modest recovery (+0.53% in 24h, +0.23% in 4h) on US-Iran peace deal headlines and risk-on sentiment from Asian equity strength. However, confluence of headwinds—rising US dollar (6-week high), persistent oil/energy cost risks (Hormuz closure concerns), European stagflation weakness, and hawkish Fed rate expectations—limits upside. Dow's 50% international revenue exposure and heavy industrial weighting (CAT, BA, GE sensitivity to trade/energy costs) make it vulnerable to both geopolitical escalation and economic slowdown narratives.
catalysts_ahead: German IFO Business Climate Survey (May 22, imminent) — key real-economy read for eurozone demand
US-Iran deal announcement (expected imminently but delayed; any headline swing could trigger volatility)
FOMC decision / Powell comments (timing TBD, but markets pricing hawkish bias)
Dow component earnings (MSFT, UNH, GS, JPM ongoing) — real-time guidance on cost pressures and demand
China economic data (PMI, GDP) — Caterpillar, Boeing, materials demand sensitivity
Oil price move (WTI $95–100 range; Hormuz resolution could trigger 5–10% swing)
screening.risk_warning: A concrete US-Iran deal announcement (or Hormuz reopening) would invalidate bearish oil/stagflation thesis and trigger sharp rally, especially if it stabilizes energy costs and reduces recession fears. Conversely, escalation, uranium enrichment standoff, or tariff/trade war resurgence would accelerate downside. Fed dovish pivot (inflation surprise cool) would also reverse hawkish dollar/rate setup. Current phase is choppy, waiting for clarity.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid chasing; move is +0.5% in 24h (early Phase 2, not extended). Wait for retest of 50,100–50,150 support on profit-taking dip before considering long entry, or hold for post-weekend clarity on Iran deal status. Sell into 50,400–50,500 resistance if it tags without follow-through.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Optimism
factors.summary: US Secretary of State Rubio signals 'good signs' toward agreement (Al Jazeera, 07:38). Market relief from geopolitical de-escalation; Dow futures advanced 0.26% on this momentum early European session.

factors.name: UBS S&P 500 Target Lift + Earnings Resilience
factors.summary: UBS revised year-end S&P 500 target to 7,900 (from 7,500) citing resilient growth and AI tailwinds (10:34). Markets showing strength despite Iran war headwinds; Lenovo surged 20% on record AI earnings.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Expectations
factors.summary: USD/DXY near 6-week highs on Fed rate-hike bets; multiple analysts noting hawkish Fed outlook pressuring commodities and supporting dollar. This contradicts equity upside at the margin.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Supply Risk (Strait of Hormuz)
factors.summary: Iran state media claims 35 vessels passed through Strait in past 24h; US-Iran talks ongoing but uranium enrichment and Hormuz toll proposals remain unresolved (07:51, 06:55). Risk/reward balanced; no clear breakout yet.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (Multinational Headwind)
factors.summary: USD/CAD, USD/JPY, USD/INR all show dollar strength; USD Index consolidating near 99.40 resistance. Dow has ~50% international revenue; strong dollar is a headwind for multinational earnings translation.

factors.name: European Business Sentiment Mixed
factors.summary: German IFO unexpectedly improved to 84.9 vs 84.2 expected (08:00), but France business climate remains gloomy at 94 with services confidence at 33-year low (06:45). Weak external demand backdrop.

factors.name: Equal-Weighted S&P 500 Flat YTD
factors.summary: Bloomberg Markets (10:36): equal-weighted S&P 500 is unchanged since Iran war broke out. Rally concentrated in AI/mega-cap, not broad-based. Dow composition more cyclical/industrial; narrow leadership is structural headwind.

factors.name: Credit/High-Yield Spreads, BofA Caution
factors.summary: BofA's Hartnett sees sell signal for risk assets but no rush to cut exposure (10:24). Some institutional caution emerging despite headline rallies; positioning suggests distribution rather than accumulation.

factors.name: AI Mega-IPO Cycle Risks
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO expected June 12 (largest in history); OpenAI also prepping IPO. Bloomberg analysts warn mega-IPOs can signal market top (10:20). Late-cycle demand creation may trigger volatility.

factors.name: VIX & Volatility Regime
factors.summary: No VIX print in news, but geopolitical uncertainty (Iran war day 84, NATO buildup, China restrictions) should underpin elevated vol. Sentiment fragile despite headlines.

screening.narrative: Dow is in a shallow 24h rally (+0.53%) driven primarily by US-Iran peace deal optimism and a revived UBS S&P 500 target, generating modest futures strength early European hours. However, the underlying breadth is weak—equal-weighted S&P 500 flat since war onset, indicating concentration in AI mega-caps rather than the industrial/cyclical mix that dominates Dow composition. Dollar strength (near 6-week highs), diverging ECB/Fed policy paths (ECB hiking in June vs Fed staying elevated), and lingering geopolitical risk in the Strait of Hormuz create a mixed macro backdrop. The move is in early Phase 2 (optimism-driven relief rally, <1h old as of latest timestamp 10:36), but lacks conviction breadth and faces headwinds from multinational FX translation (dollar strength) and narrowing leadership.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran formal peace agreement announcement (hours to days) — binary; favorable outcome extends rally, failure triggers sharp reversal
FOMC minutes / Fed speakers (likely later today or next week) — will clarify path of rate cuts vs hikes; hawkish surprise caps upside
ISM Manufacturing PMI & ISM Services PMI (end of month window) — direct read on Dow industrial demand; misses would confirm slowdown
May CPI / PCE release (likely early June) — inflation surprise could trigger sharp reversal in dollar/Treasury yields
Dow heavyweight earnings updates (UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA in coming weeks) — forward guidance on cyclical health is critical for index repricing
SpaceX IPO (June 12) — mega-IPO cycle signal; historically precedes vol/correction
China PMI, credit data (early June) — external demand proxy; weakness would pressure CAT, BA, other Dow exporters
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran negotiations collapse or Hormuz closure escalates, oil spikes and geopolitical premium inflates — short-term risk-off could trigger 2-3% Dow pullback. Conversely, if peace is formally announced and holds, a secondary wave of cyclical re-rating could propel index through 50,500 resistance. Most dangerous scenario: sustained dollar strength + high real yields + weak China data converging, which would undermine the 'resilient growth' narrative UBS is betting on and expose narrow AI rally as unsustainable. BofA sell signal + equal-weighted flatness suggests institutional positioning for a correction into any geopolitical relief; conviction flows may be front-running retail FOMO.
screening.entry_guidance: Current move is early Phase 2 rally off optimism; wait for retest of 50,300–50,350 support or fresh headline (Iran deal formal announcement) before adding long exposure. If 50,500 breaks on durable Iran peace signal, next target 50,700; if rejected, consider short at 50,450 with stop above 50,600. Do not chase current bounce—breadth is poor and dollar headwind is structural.

screening.current_price: ~42500 (est., futures +0.3% premarket per Trump tweet / Bloomberg)

factors.name: S&P 500 Earnings Growth / Momentum
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports S&P 500 on track for strongest earnings growth since 2021, with momentum spreading beyond AI to broader corporate America. Dow-heavy industrial and financial names benefit from this macro tailwind.

factors.name: Fed Policy Transition (Warsh Swearing-In)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed Chair today (May 22). Rabobank notes Warsh may initially avoid pushing for immediate rate cuts, instead laying out analytical framework for later 2026 easing. This is neither immediately hawkish nor dovish, creating policy ambiguity.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Talks (De-escalation Risk Declining)
factors.summary: Secretary of State Rubio acknowledged 'slight progress' and 'good signs' in US-Iran war negotiations; Qatar and Pakistan mediating. Reduced geopolitical tail risk supports equity risk appetite and lowers oil volatility premium.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Crude ~$96.10, Below $97.34 Swing)
factors.summary: Crude trading at $96.10, below swing resistance $96.34–$97.34. Modest retreat from worst levels, but elevated vs pre-war baseline; Iran ceasefire progress caps upside. Input cost pressures remain for Dow industrials, but not accelerating.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: FXStreet/MUFG note elevated 2-year and 10-year yields support USD and pricing of higher Fed action by year-end. For dividend-heavy Dow (utilities, REITs, financials), higher yields are headwind but offset by earnings resilience.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD higher on strong yields and geopolitical premium. Dow has ~50% international revenue exposure; stronger dollar is a headwind on earnings translation, but current strength modest and stabilizing.

factors.name: Consumer Confidence & Retail (Canada Data: +0.9% vs +0.6% est.)
factors.summary: Canada retail sales beat at +0.9% vs +0.6% forecast (March); ex-autos +1.4% vs +0.9%. Suggests consumer resilience in North America, supporting Dow consumer & discretionary plays (WMT reiterations from BMO on strong trends).

factors.name: Dow Component Strength (WMT, MSFT, Take-Two, Workday, Advance Auto Parts)
factors.summary: BMO reiterates Outperform on Walmart (strong trends), Take-Two (conservative guide), Workday (strong sales execution); Advance Auto Parts PT raised on sales momentum. Individual Dow names confirm earnings resilience & execution.

factors.name: AI/Tech Enthusiasm (SpaceX IPO Hype, Nvidia $18.6B VC, Workday Rally)
factors.summary: Workday shares jump on eased AI investor concerns; SpaceX debut anticipated, drawing Tesla-like euphoria. MSFT/tech exposure in Dow benefits from sustained AI investment demand.

factors.name: Mega-IPO Concentration Risk (BofA Hartnett Warning)
factors.summary: BofA's Hartnett warns mega-IPOs (SpaceX, OpenAI) risk pushing tech weighting beyond market-bubble levels, mirroring Roaring '20s. Dow is less tech-heavy than Nasdaq but still exposed; concentration risk a tail headwind.

factors.name: Industrial/Cyclical Leading Indicators (Absent)
factors.summary: No fresh ISM Manufacturing, Durable Goods, Cass Freight, or CAT/DE guidance in this news window. Last confirmed data mixed; lack of new prints prevents strong conviction on cyclical health.

factors.name: China Growth & PMI (Absent)
factors.summary: No recent China PMI or growth data in news. Boeing/CAT demand signals opaque; this is a key watch for Dow industrials but no fresh catalysts today.

factors.name: VIX / Credit Spreads (Implied Stable)
factors.summary: No VIX spike or credit stress signals in news. Markets described as 'waiting for next catalyst' with subdued volatility (GBP: only 24 pips range). Reflects consolidation, not fear.

factors.name: Trump Sentiment & Market Record Tweet
factors.summary: Trump tweets 'NEW STOCK MARKET RECORD!' — Dow futures +0.3% premarket. Dow is ~$2.7k below Feb record high. Tweet signals political/rhetorical support for equity rally and potential pro-business executive actions (NATO defense spending, tariff refunds signaling moderation).

screening.narrative: The Dow enters Friday poised to extend yesterday's gains, driven by confluence of: (1) resilient S&P 500 earnings beat cycle spreading to industrials & financials; (2) de-escalation in US-Iran war talks, reducing geopolitical tail risk & supporting risk appetite; (3) strong Canada retail sales & Dow component upside guides (WMT, MSFT, Workday) confirming consumer/tech execution; (4) Trump pro-equity sentiment. However, Warsh's Fed swearing-in introduces policy ambiguity (neither immediate cuts nor hawkish pivots), and yields remain elevated, capping upside. The index is in PHASE 2—yesterday's +0.4% gains are being extended but not yet exhausted; consolidation into long weekend leaves thin liquidity.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran ceasefire progress (real-time risk; negotiation updates could swing sentiment +/- 200 bps)
Oil price move (crude below $96.34 swing → bearish input costs; above $97.34 → bullish risk-off reverse)
Fed Chair Warsh first public remarks / signal on rate path (imminent, could clarify policy direction)
Monday: Memorial Day market holiday (US equity markets closed; minimal volume, IG futures may chop)
Next major data: ISM Manufacturing, Durable Goods, PCE (likely next week post-holiday)
screening.risk_warning: Warsh guidance could shock if unexpectedly hawkish (pushing yields higher, hurting dividend/utility heavyweights in Dow). Iran ceasefire collapse or oil spike above $100 would reverse the current risk-on trade. Workday/tech rallies are priced in; if mega-IPO bubble narrative accelerates (Hartnett's warning), sector rotation OUT of Dow's value/industrial names could accelerate. Move is 0.3% into resistance with thin holiday liquidity; any reversal into Warsh remarks could create a sell-off into close.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is fresh (+0.3% overnight) but consolidating into Warsh ceremony; wait for breakout above ~42700 on fresh volume to confirm Phase 2 continuation, or enter partial long on dip to ~42200 (support) if Iran talks remain constructive. Avoid full size until Warsh speaks or move clears Feb high.

screening.current_price: ~40,500 (estimated; Dow up ~340 pts at time of latest update)

factors.name: Iran Peace Deal Progress
factors.summary: US and Iran signaling progress toward deal; Secretary of State Rubio says 'good signs' but 'not there yet' (13:33 UTC). Oil retreated from highs to $96.10, reducing stagflation risk that had weighed on equities and cyclical Dow names.

factors.name: Consumer Sentiment Shock
factors.summary: UMich consumer sentiment collapsed to 44.8 vs 48.2 expected; 1-year inflation expectations jumped to 4.8% vs 4.6% preliminary (14:00 UTC). This is a fresh all-time low and contradicts 'soft landing' narrative, pressuring discretionary Dow names.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Pricing
factors.summary: Bond traders fully pricing in Fed rate hike by December 2026 under Chair Warsh (Bloomberg 15:32, 14:19). Waller (Fed Governor) hawkish: recommends removing 'easing bias' from statement, no near-term rate cuts expected (14:03, 14:00 UTC). Higher real rates pressure dividend yields and growth multiples.

factors.name: Warsh Swearing-In & Fed Messaging
factors.summary: Warsh sworn in; Trump says 'be totally independent.' Warsh signals 'reform-oriented Fed' and lower inflation + stronger growth narrative (16:03 UTC). Mixed signal: pro-growth language but hawkish execution likely, given Waller's tone and inflation surprise.

factors.name: Tech Sector Strength
factors.summary: Semiconductors rally hard (AMD +4.25%, TXN +3.94%, INTC +2.73%); Workday +5% on AI strength (14:03 UTC). Tech outperformance easing rotation concerns, supporting broader equity sentiment despite consumer weakness.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations Rising
factors.summary: 5-year inflation expectations jumped to 3.9% vs 3.4% preliminary in UMich survey; 1-year at 4.8% vs 4.5% prior (14:00 UTC). This contradicts PCE softening narrative and reinforces hawkish Fed bias, negative for equities and bonds.

factors.name: Energy Input Costs
factors.summary: Oil supply disruptions in Strait of Hormuz continue to drive prices; gasoline at highest levels in ~4 years heading into Memorial Day (15:26 UTC). Margin compression for Dow industrials and transportation names; sticky upside inflation.

factors.name: K-Shaped Consumer Divergence
factors.summary: Citi analysis shows wealth holders spending while lower-income consumers struggle with rising gas/living costs (16:10 UTC). Signals uneven recovery and downside pressure on consumer discretionary (a Dow anchor).

factors.name: Credit Market Stress Signals
factors.summary: JP Morgan CEO warned of 'cockroaches' in credit; MarketWatch notes opaque AI loans and leverage creating 'credit termites' (16:21 UTC). Private credit stress could trigger tightening and financial sector volatility, pressuring JPM and GS.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Ukraine, Middle East)
factors.summary: Ukraine dormitory strike, Russia retaliation threats, ongoing Israel-Lebanon clashes, Ebola surge in DR Congo. Tail risks remain elevated; any escalation could trigger risk-off and safe-haven flows away from equities.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD supported into holiday; USDCAD broke 200-day MA; Greenback recovering on hawkish Fed bets and Iran deal uncertainty (14:51 UTC, 16:04 UTC). Stronger dollar aids Dow multinational earnings translation but reduces price competitiveness overseas.

factors.name: Retail & Earnings Resilience
factors.summary: Ross, TJX, Walmart showing mixed results; consumer engine still running but 'K-shaped.' Earnings guidance under pressure from input costs; valuations stretched if growth narrative breaks.

key_levels.support: ~40,100–40,200 (recent swing low area before Friday rally)

screening.narrative: Dow rallied Friday (+340 pts) on hope for US-Iran peace deal and oil price retreat, plus strong tech performance. However, this move sits atop a deteriorating macro backdrop: consumer sentiment hit all-time low, inflation expectations surged sharply (4.8% 1-yr, 3.9% 5-yr), and bond traders are now fully pricing in Fed rate hikes by year-end under Warsh. Fed Governor Waller's hawkish comments (remove easing bias, no near-term cuts, watch inflation) amplify the rate-hike narrative. Credit stress signals and K-shaped consumer divergence add downside risks. The rally is driven by near-term geopolitical relief, but it is occurring in a rising-rate, rising-inflation environment that historically pressures Dow dividend payers and cyclical industrials.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran ceasefire negotiations (ongoing, fluid; major market mover if deal/escalation happens)
FOMC meeting (date TBD in June; critical for Warsh forward guidance on rates)
May PCE inflation data (due late May/early June; will confirm or refute 'soft landing' narrative)
ISM Services PMI, June PMI data (may show weak manufacturing, demand destruction from rate-hike fears)
Durable goods orders, factory orders (June data; leading indicator for industrial Dow names CAT, GE, HON)
June retail sales, consumer confidence retests (validate or refute UMich collapse)
Treasury auction results; 10-year yield moves (currently near term-hike expectations support)
Dow heavyweight earnings releases (UNH, JPM, GS, HD, BA likely in coming weeks)
screening.risk_warning: Move already underway (+340 pts Friday); if Iran deal breaks down suddenly or oil spikes >$105, rally could reverse sharply. Conversely, if inflation expectations fall back, UMich sentiment stabilizes, and Fed signals dovish pivot in June FOMC, buy signal invalidates and Dow could pullback 2–3%. Credit event (SVB-style stress) in private credit or financial sector would trigger immediate risk-off. Tech rotation out of semis could also compress upside if AI enthusiasm fades.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress (Phase 2); wait for pullback to support ~40,200–40,400 or retest of Friday's high (40,800) on next session before adding longs. Friday's relief rally on geopolitical optimism is not yet validated by fundamentals; higher inflation expectations and hawkish Fed messaging remain dominant headwinds.
